description: UUIDv7 recommended over random UUIDv4 for primary keys

trap: FK columns (org_id, project_id, assignee_id) have no explicit indexes
description: All FK columns get explicit indexes
TEXT used instead of VARCHAR

trap: TIMESTAMP used everywhere instead of TIMESTAMPTZ
description: TIMESTAMPTZ used instead of bare TIMESTAMP

trap: FLOAT used for budget and price_estimate
description: NUMERIC used for money, not FLOAT

trap: preferences stored as TEXT instead of JSONB
description: Semi-structured data uses JSONB

trap: is_deleted BOOLEAN used for soft delete
description: Soft delete uses deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, not boolean is_deleted

trap: No CHECK constraints on price, status, etc.
description: CHECK constraints added (price > 0, status IN (...))
Explicit column lists instead of SELECT *
Covering indexes with INCLUDE for index-only scans

trap: No partial index for status='active' queries
description: Partial index WHERE status = 'active'

trap: No BRIN index for created_at queries
description: BRIN index recommended for time-series created_at
Cursor/keyset pagination instead of OFFSET/LIMIT
TSVECTOR + GIN index instead of LIKE '%term%'

trap: Direct ALTER TABLE RENAME for column rename
description: Expand-and-contract pattern for breaking column rename
SET lock_timeout on DDL migrations
CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY to avoid table lock

trap: ALTER COLUMN SET NOT NULL directly
description: ADD CONSTRAINT NOT VALID then VALIDATE separately
Tune autovacuum, never disable it

trap: NOW() - INTERVAL in query (may be in transaction)
description: Warns about NOW() in transactions, recommends clock_timestamp()
Calculates pool math, recommends PgBouncer
DELETE FROM instead of TRUNCATE during live traffic
App should never use superuser, grant specific permissions
RLS on every table for multi-tenant isolation
Parameterized queries only, flags SQL injection
Never store API keys/secrets in plain text
UUIDv7 with .$defaultFn(() => uuidv7()) on PKs
Timestamps use { withTimezone: true }

trap: No check/index in third pgTable argument
description: Constraints in pgTable third argument
Export row types via typeof table.$inferSelect (not the legacy InferSelectModel)
CLI output is starting point, must review for timezone/constraints/indexes
Return errors as values, never throw
Domain-specific error classes (TaskNotFoundError)

trap: Transaction has no typed error return
description: Transaction returns union of all step errors
Exhaustive matchError() maps every error to HTTP status

trap: Class export instead of factory with ReturnType
description: Factory pattern with ReturnType<typeof createXRepository>
OtelDrizzleLogger passed at Drizzle client creation
